rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TIM HURDLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/01/2026. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to list petitioner's telephone number at item 2 of acknowledgment of receipt on page 2. 3) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) According to petitioner's supplemental declaration filed on 04/28/2026, the decedent died testate. If so: i) An amended petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary must be filed with a copy of the will attached to the petition as Attachment 3f(2). ii) The decedent's original will must be delivered to the clerk's office. 6) If Louise Hurdle is the decedent's spouse, survived the decedent, and died testate: A) Please provide the name and address of her personal representative with a copy of letters issued to the representative. Notice must also be given to the representative. B) If there is no personal representative, please provide the names and addresses of all devisees named in her will. Notice must also be given to each devisee. 6) If Chester Hurdle is the decedent's predeceased brother, is Ken Hurdle the only issue of Chester Hurdle? 7) If Hannah Toomey is the decedent's sister and died, please provide her date of death and: i) If she predeceased, is Judith Wesley the only issue of Hannah Toomey? ii) If she survived the decedent, please provide the name and address of her personal representative with a copy of letters issued to the representative. Notice must also be given to the representative. If there is no personal representative: A) Did she die intestate? If so, please provide the names and addresses of her heirs including her surviving spouse or registered domestic partner if applicable. Notice must also be given to each heir. B) Did she die testate? If so, please provide the names and addresses of all devisees named in her will. Notice must also be given to each devisee. 8) Please provide the names and dates of death of petitioner's parent who was the decedent's nibling and petitioner's grandparent who was the decedent's sibling.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALLURA FONG SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #15, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of the additional bond ordered on 3/20/26 has not been filed yet. (2) The Court Investigator's report will need to be completed and proof of payment of the assessment fee filed before this accounting can be approved. (3) The US Bank account statement ends 12/11/25 and shows a different balance from the balance shown in the accounting. No reconciliation is shown. The statement should encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period. (4) Schedule F does not appear to be entirely correct. First, there is no total shown for the shares reinvested, but the number of Franklin Templeton shares added to the shares that were on hand as of 12/31/24 do not appear to total 372.616. (5) There is no schedule of gains or losses, but the Fidelity Equity Dividend Income mutual fund was apparently redeemed for a higher value than the value of the 2,590.851 shares on hand as of 12/31/24 plus the 9.867 shares reinvested on 4/5/24. If there are no shares remaining, the difference carried forward since 1999 should be shown as a "loss." (6) Home Care disbursements should be a separate category from Living Expenses, so that the figure the co-trustees rely on to argue for their caregiving fee is clear. (7) Did it really take the attorney a full hour each time on 2/18/26, 2/25/26, and 2/26/26 to review the paralegal's work? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/15/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required for the Court to determine if a request for fees for the co-conservators' attorney is appropriately made in the trust. (In interim accountings, it is not necessary to file financial account statements other than the ones covering the ending date of the accounting period. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2). It makes more work for the court to review, too.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT E. O'DONNELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "supplement" e-filed on 4/21/26 AMENDED the petition. An amending pleading requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] An AMENDMENT to the petition must be filed. Notice of the 7/1/26 hearing of the petition (showing the full, correct caption of the petition) and the amendment (after it is e-filed and viewable on the court's website) must be served on all interested parties. The amendment must also address other, following issues. (2) The calculation of the proposed distributions to the beneficiaries of the pecuniary bequests shows that $10,500 + 365 = $27.76. $10,500 + 365 = $28.77. (3) The Gains on Sales amount reported on the Summary of Account is not supported by the schedule. (4) Amounts reimbursed to the executor must show who the original payee was. (5) Costs paid to the executor's attorney must also be itemized and clearly indicate what they were for (what was filed and when)? (6) Refunds for amounts paid during administration are not receipts includable in the fee base. (7) Attachment 8 to the Petition for Probate indicated that Decedent's brother, Paul O'Donnell, was survived by three children, not just Alexandra Kirkland and Paul O'Donnell III. If Teresa Mumbrue is one of his children, distribution to her should be her share + 1/3 of her father's share. (8) When did Decedent's brother, James Joseph O'Donnell, Jr., die? Attachment 8 to the Petition for Probate indicated that he died on 5/3/16, not survived by issue, but Executor now alleges that he was survived issue (Mary Agnes Olivella). (9) It appears that the executor also liquidated all of the securities under his IAEA authority. (10) There were two Corrected I&As filed. (11) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (amendment and proof of service and bank statements) to the assigned examiner by 6/17/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It would be helpful if an accounting included a detailed schedule of assets on hand at the start of the accounting period. The creditors' claims period expired on 5/25/24, four months after Letters were issued, not 9/18/24. The summary of account shown on page 7 of the petition shows the wrong totals. No 13100 affidavit is required for issue taking due to a predeceased beneficiary. A correct 13100 affidavit must include the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-300 and must be filed conventionally with a certified copy of the death certificate attached. Any document in a foreign language just be accompanied by a translation. [LRSF 14.15 D.]) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL D. BRIARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/22/26 to follow outcome of the 11700 petition. Courtesy copies of any new filed papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 6/8/26. (Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.]) If the petition must be amended, and 15 days' notice of the amendment (or fully amended petition) cannot be served for the 6/22/26 hearing, Counsel should contact the examiner to request a further continuance. NB: Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] "Filing" means when the pleading requiring notice appears on the Court's register of actions (and becomes publicly viewable in decedent estate and trust cases). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEHA MALLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Ownership And To Compel Return Of Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 01/13/2026): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice, if the matter becomes contested. See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). Proof of service must be filed as a separate document (and not as an attachment to the proposed order merely lodged). 2) No DE-115 form is on file. Copies of the petition and remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be served with the form. 3) As to each property subject to this petition, other than the decedent's retirement accounts: i) Was each asset disclosed to the decedent's former spouse during the divorce proceeding? If any asset was undisclosed community property, does section XX of the marital settlement agreement apply? ii) Was each asset omitted community property requiring a post-judgment motion under Family Code section 2556 to divide? iii) Was each asset acquired by the decedent after signing the marital settlement agreement? If not, please provide facts in support of the separate property characterization. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SOPHIA HSUEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 11/22/2027.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GREGORY MICHAEL PENNINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 11/15/27. The co-administrators must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of a second report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 11/15/27. Appearance will be required on 11/15/27 if no petition has been filed.
